response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, craftsmanship-oriented senior engineer with genuine language-tooling interest — he worked on NI LabWindows/CVI (a C IDE) and authored test runners and assertion libraries — but his core stack is TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, not the JVM. Java appears only peripherally, and there is no Kotlin or Spring experience at all, which are hard requirements for a role centered on enterprise Java/Kotlin IDE features. Strong engineering fundamentals partially offset the stack mismatch, but he would need significant ramp-up on the exact ecosystem the role tools for.
strengths: Direct IDE development experience (NI LabWindows/CVI) plus authored developer tooling (Trial test runner, Fluent-Asserts)
Deep software craftsmanship background: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, testing workshops and courses
Proven experience on complex, high-scale systems (low-latency services handling millions of requests/sec)
concerns: No Kotlin and no Spring/enterprise JVM framework experience — the role's core required stack
Java exposure is minimal and dated (side usage at Altom, 2016-2018), far from 'deep JVM understanding'
Recent work (TypeScript/Node, AI tooling) diverges from enterprise Java tooling; current career gap adds ramp-up risk

key_factors.factor: IDE internals and language tooling experience
Strong testing/craftsmanship and large-scale systems background
